title: The Ultimate 10 Herbal Teas for Acid Reflux Healing
intro_desc: Herbal teas for acid reflux are natural remedies that help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux by reducing inflammation, soothing the digestive system, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal teas for acid reflux include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinchona officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Silybum marianum, Camellia sinensis, Ginkgo biloba, Matricaria chamomilla, and Aloe barbadensis. If left untreated, acid reflux can lead to chronic discomfort, sleep disturbances, and even impact daily activities, social life, and relationships.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to help with acid reflux. One reason it's effective is because of its anti-inflammatory properties. Ginger helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, which can cause the muscles to relax and allow stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus. This can lead to symptoms like heartburn and discomfort. Ginger also has a soothing effect on the digestive system, helping to calm down the stomach and reduce acid production. Additionally, ginger has natural antacid properties that can help neutralize stomach acid and provide quick relief from acid reflux symptoms. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work together to provide these benefits and help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for acid reflux because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the esophagus and ease digestive discomfort.
Shogaols are good for acid reflux because they help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and prevent acid production, which can help to alleviate symptoms of heartburn and indigestion.
Polyphenols are good for acid reflux because they help to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate the symptoms of heartburn and acid indigestion.
Zingerone is good for acid reflux because it helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and relax the muscles that allow stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus.
Tannins, also known as chamomile, are good for acid reflux because they help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, thereby easing symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for acid reflux
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea that helps with acid reflux, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers, which can be bought from a health food store or online. Use one teaspoon of dried flowers for every cup of boiling water. Next, boil a cup of water and carefully pour it over the dried flowers in a teapot or mug. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help release the herbal properties into the water. After the steeping time is up, you can strain the tea by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids. Finally, drink the tea slowly and calmly, ideally 30 minutes before meals to help prevent acid reflux. This herbal tea can be enjoyed warm or at room temperature and is best consumed in moderation, ideally 1-2 cups per day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a popular natural remedy for acid reflux. The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach. This can provide relief from the burning sensation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. The licorice root also contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract. By reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membranes, glycyrrhiza glabra tea can help to calm the symptoms of acid reflux and allow the stomach to heal. Additionally, the tea has been shown to increase the production of mucus, which can help to protect the stomach lining from acid damage and reduce the symptoms of acid reflux. Drinking glycyrrhiza glabra tea may also help to relax the muscles in the stomach and lower esophagus, which can help to prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing acid reflux.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for acid reflux because it helps to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
Flavonoids are good for acid reflux because they help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, which can help to ease symptoms of acid reflux.
Polyphenols are good for acid reflux because they help reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining, preventing the symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for acid reflux
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea that helps with acid reflux, start by gathering a handful of dried chamomile flowers. You can purchase these at a health food store or online. Next, place the dried flowers in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag, and steep them in a cup of boiling water for about 5-7 minutes. Allow the tea to cool for a minute or two before straining it into a cup. You can then add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and a bit of honey to taste. It's essential to drink the tea slowly and in small sips to help soothe the stomach and reduce acid reflux symptoms. As you drink the tea, sit comfortably and avoid lying down to allow the herbal remedy to work effectively. Chamomile tea has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the digestive system and provide relief from acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a plant used to make tea. The tea is good for acid reflux because it has soothing properties that help calm the stomach. When you drink Foeniculum vulgare tea, it can relax the muscles in your stomach and reduce the amount of acid it produces. This makes it easier to digest food and reduces the feeling of burning and discomfort in your chest that comes with acid reflux. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the esophagus and stomach. By drinking Foeniculum vulgare tea regularly, you may find that your acid reflux symptoms decrease and you can eat and drink without feeling uncomfortable.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for acid reflux because they help reduce inflammation in the stomach, relax the muscles that control digestion, and soothe the lining of the esophagus to prevent heartburn and discomfort.
Anethole helps with acid reflux because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, allowing for a decrease in acid production and discomfort.
Flavonoids are good for acid reflux because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract and reduce the production of stomach acid.
Tannins are good for acid reflux because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.

remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal teas for acid reflux
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal teas that help with acid reflux, you will need dried chamomile flowers, a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup, and a pot of boiling water. Start by adding one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to the tea infuser or directly into the heat-resistant cup. Next, pour boiling water over the chamomile flowers, making sure the water level is above the flowers. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help the chamomile flowers release their beneficial compounds. After the steeping time has passed, you can strain the tea by lifting the tea infuser or carefully pouring the tea into another cup through a fine-mesh sieve. Once the tea is strained, you can drink it hot or cold, depending on your preference. It is recommended to drink the tea 30 minutes to an hour before meals to help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, can help with acid reflux. The main reason is that peppermint oil in the tea helps relax the muscles in the stomach and the esophagus. When these muscles are relaxed, they don't put as much pressure on the stomach acid, which can reduce the burning sensation in the chest. Peppermint tea also helps to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can help to ease the discomfort of acid reflux. Additionally, the cooling sensation of peppermint tea can help to numb the pain and make it easier to swallow. Some people find that drinking a cup of peppermint tea before eating can help to reduce symptoms of acid reflux, as it can help to calm the stomach and prepare it for digestion.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for acid reflux because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the throat.

Quercetin is good for acid reflux because it helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus and relaxes the lower esophageal sphincter, which can help prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the throat.
Luteolin is good for acid reflux because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and protect it from acid damage.
Tannins are good for acid reflux because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation in the esophagus and ease symptoms such as heartburn and indigestion.
Catechins are good for acid reflux because they help relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can ease symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for acid reflux
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea that helps with acid reflux, start by gathering 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers. You can purchase these at a health food store or online. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop, then reduce the heat to a gentle simmer. Add the dried chamomile flowers to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag, and gently submerge it in the simmering water. Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the chamomile's calming properties to infuse into the water. Once the tea has steeped, remove the infuser or muslin bag, and carefully pour the tea into a cup. You can add a small amount of honey to sweeten the tea, if desired. Finally, drink the tea slowly and peacefully, feeling the soothing effects of Matricaria chamomilla on your digestive system and helping to alleviate acid reflux symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tea, also known as Peruvian bark, has been used to help with acid reflux for many years. The main reason it's good for acid reflux is due to its ability to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation. The tea contains a compound called quinine, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract. This relaxation helps to reduce the amount of acid that's produced in the stomach, which in turn helps to ease the symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the esophagus and reduce discomfort caused by acid reflux. When consumed, the tea can also help to improve digestion and reduce symptoms such as heartburn and bloating. This makes it a popular natural remedy for people who experience acid reflux, as it can be consumed hot or cold and is easy to incorporate into a daily routine.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quinic acid is good for acid reflux because it helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach and relaxes the muscles, which in turn helps to ease the discomfort and pain caused by acid reflux.

Quercetin is good for acid reflux because it helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can become irritated by stomach acid and cause discomfort.
Quinine is good for acid reflux because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach, preventing the acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea that helps with acid reflux, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers. Use one teaspoon of dried flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water. Next, heat the water in a kettle or pot, then pour it over the dried chamomile flowers in a cup or teapot. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their healing properties into the water. After the steeping time has passed, strain the tea into another cup to remove the flowers. You can do this by pouring the tea through a strainer or cheesecloth. Finally, drink the tea while it's still warm, ideally 30 minutes to an hour before meals to help prevent acid reflux symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is believed to help with acid reflux because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the digestive system. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids, which are powerful antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus. This can help ease the symptoms of acid reflux, such as heartburn and discomfort after eating. Ginkgo biloba tea may also help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing acid reflux. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba tea may help improve blood flow to the digestive system, which can help the body heal and repair any damage caused by acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal teas that help with acid reflux, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers from a trusted source. Begin by measuring one to two teaspoons of the dried flowers for every 8 ounces of water you plan to use. Next, boil fresh water in a kettle or on the stovetop, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Place the dried chamomile flowers in a tea infuser or directly into a teapot, and pour the hot water over them. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the desired strength of flavor is reached. Once the tea has steeped, carefully pour it into a cup and strain the solids, if needed. Finally, add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink the tea slowly to help alleviate acid reflux symptoms. It is recommended to drink the tea after meals to help soothe the stomach and reduce discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum tea, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, is a popular herbal remedy for acid reflux. The active compounds in this tea, such as cinnamaldehyde and cinnamic acid, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach. This inflammation can lead to acid reflux symptoms like heartburn and bloating. The tea also has a soothing effect on the digestive system, helping to calm down the muscles in the esophagus and prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the throat. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum tea has been shown to increase the production of mucus in the stomach, which helps to neutralize stomach acid and prevent acid reflux. Drinking Cinnamomum verum tea after meals may also help to reduce symptoms of acid reflux by slowing down the digestion process and preventing a sudden rush of stomach acid into the esophagus.

Polyphenols are good for acid reflux because they can help calm the stomach and reduce inflammation, allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus at a slower rate, thereby providing relief from heartburn and discomfort.

Flavonoids are good for acid reflux because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea that helps with acid reflux, start by gathering high-quality dried chamomile flowers. Begin by boiling fresh water in a kettle or pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Pour the hot water over the chamomile flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their calming properties. After the steeping time, carefully remove the tea infuser or cup from the heat and let it cool slightly. Strain the tea into a separate container to remove the flowers, if using loose-leaf chamomile. Once strained, your tea is ready to drink. To help with acid reflux, drink 1-2 cups of the chamomile tea 30 minutes to 1 hour before meals, and again before bedtime to promote a good night's sleep. As with any herbal remedy, consult with a healthcare professional before using Matricaria chamomilla tea, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking any medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum tea, also known as prickly ash tea, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to help soothe and calm the digestive system. The tea is made from the bark of the Zanthoxylum bungeanum tree, which contains a compound called caryophyllene. This compound has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, a common cause of acid reflux. When we eat, the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) helps to keep food from flowing back up into the esophagus. But in people with acid reflux, the LES can be weakened, allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus. The caryophyllene in Zanthoxylum bungeanum tea may help to strengthen the LES, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Additionally, the tea has been shown to have a soothing effect on the stomach, reducing the production of stomach acid and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and bloating.

Flavonoids are good for acid reflux because they help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and relax the muscles that can cause stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus.

Isorhapontigenin is good for acid reflux because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.

Begin by boiling one cup of water in a pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their medicinal properties. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth, and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to use a small amount of honey as it can stimulate the stomach and make acid reflux worse. Once your tea is ready, let it cool down for a few minutes before drinking it slowly, ideally before meals to help prevent acid reflux symptoms. Drinking chamomile tea after a meal can also help to alleviate symptoms of heartburn and indigestion, making it a soothing and natural remedy for acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tea, also known as turmeric tea, is a natural remedy for acid reflux. The main ingredient, curcumin, is a powerful antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. This inflammation can cause stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus, leading to acid reflux symptoms like heartburn and discomfort. Curcumin in turmeric tea has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing the production of stomach acid and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, curcumin has a natural anti-spasmodic effect, which helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, preventing food from flowing back up into the esophagus. By drinking turmeric tea, you can reduce the frequency and severity of acid reflux attacks, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a healthy digestive system.

Polyphenols, also known as chamomile, are good for acid reflux because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and ease symptoms such as heartburn and discomfort.

remedies.image_alt: Matricaria chamomilla herbal teas for acid reflux
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea for acid reflux, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers, a tea infuser, and a cup. Begin by boiling water in a kettle, then pour it into the tea infuser. Add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to the infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. As the flowers steep, the water will turn a light yellow color and release the herb's soothing properties. Once the steeping time is complete, carefully lift the infuser out of the cup and discard the used flowers. Strain the tea into another cup if necessary, and your Matricaria chamomilla tea is ready to drink. For acid reflux relief, drink this tea after meals or before bedtime to help calm the stomach and reduce discomfort. You can also add a bit of honey to sweeten the tea if you prefer.

supplementary_best: For acid reflux relief, a blend of Foeniculum vulgare (fennel), Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root), and Zingiber officinale (ginger) is effective. Fennel helps to relax the lower esophageal sphincter and ease digestion. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach lining and reduce acid production. Ginger, with its natural anti-inflammatory compounds, calms the digestive system and alleviates nausea. This combination works well together: fennel's relaxation properties complement licorice root's anti-inflammatory effects, while ginger's digestive benefits enhance the overall soothing effect. Drinking this blend after meals may help to alleviate acid reflux symptoms and promote a smoother digestive process.
supplementary_avoid: If you have acid reflux, it's best to avoid certain herbal teas that can irritate your stomach and worsen symptoms. One such tea is peppermint tea, as it can relax the lower esophageal sphincter and allow stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus. Another tea to avoid is dandelion tea, which may stimulate digestion and increase acid production in the stomach. Yerba mate tea can also be problematic, as it contains caffeine, which can relax the lower esophageal sphincter and worsen acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, licorice root tea should be avoided, as it contains glycyrrhetic acid, which can increase the risk of acid reflux by slowing down digestion and increasing stomach acid production. Lastly, passionflower tea can be a problem, as it can slow down digestion and worsen acid reflux symptoms. These teas may exacerbate acid reflux symptoms and should be avoided if you experience frequent heartburn or acid reflux.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera juice is a natural remedy that can help with acid reflux. It works by coating the esophagus and reducing inflammation, making it easier for stomach acid to move back down into the stomach. This can provide quick relief from symptoms like heartburn and discomfort. Some people also find that ginger, consumed in the form of juice or candies, can help ease acid reflux. Ginger has a natural anti-inflammatory effect that can reduce swelling in the esophagus and help food move more smoothly through the digestive system. Additionally, a person's diet can play a big role in managing acid reflux. Eating smaller, more frequent meals can help reduce pressure on the stomach and prevent acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Probiotics, which are good bacteria found in foods like yogurt and kefir, can also help regulate digestion and reduce symptoms of acid reflux. These beneficial bacteria can help keep the digestive system in balance and prevent overgrowth of bad bacteria that can contribute to acid reflux.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with acid reflux often experience other health issues due to the same underlying causes. They may have trouble swallowing or feel like food is stuck in their throat, which can be relieved with marshmallow root. This herb soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow and reducing inflammation.  They may also experience heartburn, which is often caused by a condition called hiatal hernia. Aloe vera can help heal and protect the lining of the stomach, reducing inflammation and pain.  Some people with acid reflux may have allergies or sensitive stomachs, which can make their symptoms worse. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an irritated stomach and reduce inflammation.  People with acid reflux may also experience digestive issues like bloating, gas, or diarrhea. Dandelion root can help stimulate digestion, relieve bloating, and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.  Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an irritated stomach and reduce nausea. It can also help stimulate digestion and relieve bloating and gas.  Slippery elm has demulcent properties that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort.  Marshmallow root, aloe vera, licorice root, dandelion root, ginger, and slippery elm can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux and other related health issues.
